sections.paragraph:
an unused root. Arab. جَارَ Med. Ye appears to have signified to be hot, to boil up; whence جَاِيءرُ heat of the breast from anger, hunger, thirst. To this answers gähren, in other dialects gohren, giehren. Hence-

גיר

, גִּר m. lime, so called from its effervescing when slacked, Isa 27:9. Arab. جيْرُ and جَيَّارُ quick-lime.

גיר

Ch. emphat. גִּירָא id. Dan 5:5 compare Isa 27:9 Amo 2:1, Targ.
